sections.paragraph: Tirhakah, pr.n. of a king of Ethiopia and Thebais, Isa 37:9 2Ki 19:9 Τεάρκων of Strabo (xv. 1, § 6), Τάρκος or Ταρακός of Manethon (ap. Syncellum, v. Routhii Rell. Sacræ, ii. p. 46; compare my Comment. on Isa 18:1). Salt supposes that he found this name written in Hieroglyphico-phonetic letters, T-h-r-k, on Egyptian monuments; see Rühle v. Lilienstern, Graphische Darstellungen aus der alten Geschichte, i. 98.
